title: Swida × dubia
intro: Swida × dubia, also known as Cornelian cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its bright red berries and fragrant flowers. The health benefits of Swida × dubia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Swida × dubia include teas, tinctures, and dried extracts, often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Swida × dubia is a hybrid species within the genus Swida, commonly known as the American dogwood. It is a cross between two species of dogwood, but the exact parent species are not well-documented. This hybrid is often cultivated for its attractive foliage and showy flowers. Swida × dubia is typically classified within the family Cornaceae and is native to North America. The classification of this species is subject to ongoing research and may be refined as more information becomes available.
uses: Swida × dubia is a shrub with a long history of use in traditional medicine, particularly in Asia. The plant's berries and leaves are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. It is also used as an anti-inflammatory and to treat respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Additionally, Swida × dubia is believed to have antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.
properties: Swida × dubia is known to have various therapeutic properties. Its extracts have been found to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which can help to protect against cell damage, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections. Additionally, Swida × dubia has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and digestive issues. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential to inhibit cancer cell growth and improve immune function. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Swida × dubia.
